hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The solver must read C modules, run the matrix, and compare audit steps to scripted plans to find which invariant breaks.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Correct output requires coordinating boundary tags, intrusive free-list wiring, relocation copies, accounting tallies, and JSON emission.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The prompt reports audit drift under stress without naming tag/footer rules, coalesce direction, or copy bounds.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Fixing only list linking can leave split metadata or byte counts wrong for other scenarios.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Custom implicit-list invariants with coupled accounting are not a textbook malloc drop-in.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest public contract does not reveal internal splice or tag rules.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Six scenario scripts are checked, not one secret corrupt file.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Flipping contract spreads failures across four module roots.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests cover split, coalesce, in-place and relocating realloc, and mixed stress.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Symptoms-only wording still leaves diagnosis to runtime and code.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is invariant reasoning, not anti-tamper alone.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Core work is semantic allocator repair.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No single function flips a majority of tests.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle patches multiple C modules with substantive logic.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Not solvable by one config or table edit.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns are banned from fix-path symbol names.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix symbols are opaque; decoys rhyme structurally.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Standard pool allocator recipes fail on coupled accounting and tag/footer coupling.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security framing for hardness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tag, list, reloc, and tally requirements interact.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Shell matrix provides realism; hardness is heap semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires tracing multi-module mutations across scenarios.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Combined invariant debugging is clearly hard.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Four discoveries enumerated with planned locations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Planned instruction is symptoms-only.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three topologies each need three or more coordinated locations.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Deterministic scripts and recomputed heap signatures are programmatically checkable.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Output path and audit fields can be stated without overspecifying internals.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can repair the small C harness in a few hours.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Requires professional heap and intrusive-list debugging.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Allocator invariant drift is operationally valuable.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Tests grade audit JSON and ledger, not process.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Instruction alone does not specify footer placement, bidirectional coalesce, relocation copy size, or tally update order.

discovery_budget.discovery: Splitting must update both header and footer size words for each new piece.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming footer placement reveals the tag repair site.

discovery_budget.discovery: Freeing must merge with both adjacent free neighbors, not only the next block.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating bidirectional coalesce gives the list repair away.

discovery_budget.discovery: Relocation must copy the payload span of the old block, not a short or header-only span.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Copy bounds would pinpoint the reloc helper.

discovery_budget.discovery: Byte tallies must not double-count split remainders when adjusting live allocated bytes.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming double-count behavior exposes the accounting fix.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The instruction describes audit drift and observable JSON fields without tag algorithms, list splice rules, or repair causes.

attack_path.summary: Run matrix, compare failing scenario steps to plan scripts, trace heap mutations in C modules, coordinate tag/list/reloc/acct repairs, rebuild, re-run.
attack_path.reasoning: Requires code tracing and invariant reconstruction, not prompt transcription.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Repair footer tagging on split, bidirectional coalesce linking, relocation copy size, and split-aware accounting across four C files.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any smaller change leaves independent tests failing on signatures, free-list shape, or byte totals.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Distributed opaque roots, plan-derived expectations, and no golden audit in environment.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: After the honest contract, the solver must still reconstruct implicit-list invariants and align emission with recomputed models.

topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Split orchestration calls tag helpers; wrong footers break jade signatures while list logic may still look plausible.
Coalesce interacts with split remainders and tally updates; slate fails on free-region shape even if tags look locally consistent.
Move helper, runner, and reporting must agree on payload integrity; onyx fails while cedar may pass.

symbol_table.purpose: Writes boundary size words for a block span.
Splices a free block into the intrusive list.
Splits an allocated block and returns the remainder free piece.
Adjusts live byte tallies after heap mutations.
Writes JSON audit and companion ledger for one scenario.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Buckets small integers for documentation tables.
Formats stderr diagnostics not used in the audit path.

renames_during_drafting.reason: Original name collided with instruction noun block.
Original name collided with instruction nouns free and list.
Original name collided with instruction noun split.
Original name collided with instruction noun relocation.
Original name collided with instruction noun accounting.
